projects.subtitle: Live Music Video Production
projects.title: Koda Kosmos live on tape
projects.credits: Florian Seefing – Lighting Design/Operating, Daniel Olbricht – Musician
projects.description: A live multi-camera production at Landschaftspark Duisburg-Nord, where I designed and operated a real-time pixel mapping system for 60+ wireless LED tubes driven by the duo's live synthesizer performance. Visuals were generated and controlled in real time using TAPE, a custom tool I built to bridge Ableton Live and TouchDesigner. Video to follow.

projects.role: Light Designer, Creative Direction, TouchDesigner Developer
projects.subtitle: Audiovisual Lightshow
projects.title: ELEO
projects.description: With a unique fusion of electronic club music and gentle vocals, ELEO creates a sonic world where every emotion finds its place. With self-developed pixel tubes, beam bars, and mirrors, the music becomes a live visual experience. Innovative lighting work illuminates expansive synthesizer sounds in detail-oriented productions, turning the performance into a multisensory experience.

projects.subtitle: Fulldome Performance
projects.title: &#X1UFFFE
projects.credits: Emil Theodor Felhofer – Direction, dance, sound. Sasha Krutikova – Dance, sound, choreography
projects.description:
A symbiosis of dance, sound, sensory experience, and visuals.
Sound and visuals respond in real time to the dancers, driven by body sensors.

projects.subtitle: Audiovisual Performance
projects.title: manipulation
projects.description:
"manipulation" takes a DNA sequence as its starting point. Through hand movements, the performer intervenes directly in the audiovisual system — shaping sound and image in real time. The work traces the tension between biological structure and digital logic, asking what it means to influence something you yourself have set in motion.
Made with Ableton, TouchDesigner and Leapmotion Sensor

projects.subtitle: Interactive Art Installation
projects.title: (Prototype) Plant Designer
projects.credits: Project by: Konrad Simon, Mathias Wensauer, Marika Ajima, Tobia Diekmann
projects.description:
In this interactive installation, visitors can explore and create personalized organic geo-metries based on three different L-system algorithms. L-systems (Lindenmayer systems)
are a mathematical model used to describe the growth of plants and other natural forms through recursive rules and geometric patterns.

Using rotary controls and sensors, participants manipulate parameters to generate unique plant-inspired forms, which can be printed as tangible artworks. The installation encourages playful exploration of algorithmic design and the intersection of nature and technology.

Made with TouchDesginer and Arduino
